chief_complaint: A 10-year-old male presented with neck pain and limited range of motion.
history: The patient was diagnosed with Mycoplasma pneumoniae infection 2 weeks prior to presentation. He had been experiencing worsening neck pain and stiffness over the past 3 days. He denied any history of trauma or recent illness. He had no known allergies and was not taking any medications.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 37.5°C, blood pressure 100/60 mmHg, pulse 100 beats per minute. Physical examination revealed limited range of motion in the neck, tenderness to palpation over the atlanto-axial joint, and no neurological deficits.
labs: 3D reconstructed computed tomography of the cervical spine showed non-traumatic rotational subluxation of the atlanto-axial joint.
correct_diagnosis: Grisel syndrome following Mycoplasma pneumoniae infection
differentials: Grisel syndrome following Mycoplasma pneumoniae infection
correct_ranking: Grisel syndrome following Mycoplasma pneumoniae infection
